display_name: 前生物同手性起源机制
benchmark_scope: Construct a complete prebiotic mechanism achieving |ee| > 0.999 from ee_0 = 0 under early-Earth conditions. The mechanism must explain the source of initial symmetry breaking AND its amplification to near-complete homochirality, with full kinetic analysis. No single known mechanism is sufficient; a novel combination or new mechanism is required.
planning_constraints: The Frank model requires ee_0 != 0 as input; the mechanism must explain the source of initial chiral bias from a racemic state.
Do not assume any single known mechanism (CPL, Soai, Viedma, PVED) is sufficient; each has documented fundamental limitations.
CPL alone: anisotropy factor g ~ 0.01 places a hard physical ceiling far below 99.9% ee.
Soai reaction: requires anhydrous organozinc conditions not available prebiotically.
Viedma ripening: achieves crystal-level homochirality only, not molecular-level amino acid ee.
Any multi-step sequential mechanism must show environmental compatibility between successive steps.
Kinetic analysis must quantify the minimum initial ee_0 for Frank-type amplification and the timescale to reach 0.999.
